PnL by 30m BB Squeeze Ratio at Entry (parent-level)

Current 30-min Bollinger Band width % divided by its trailing 10-day mean (configured via BB_WIDTH_AVG_LOOKBACK_DAYS). < 1 = bands tighter than usual at entry (compression / squeeze). > 1 = bands wider than usual at entry (already in expansion). Tests whether trades fired during a squeeze run differently than trades fired during expansion.

30m BB squeeze ratioSetupsMean %Median %Win %Net P&L

PnL by 30m BB Width vs Daily ATR at Entry (parent-level)

Ratio of current 30-min Bollinger Band width % to daily ATR %. < 1 = the 30-min bands are narrower than the typical daily range (intraday compressed against a wider daily envelope). > 1 = the 30-min bands are wider than the daily ATR (intraday already expanded relative to daily volatility).

PnL by Exit Reason

The engine writes a single ema_exit reason for every EMA-trail exit; this table splits it into EMA8 Trail (close < EMA8) (active on L3 by default and on surviving siblings after L1 fills) and EMA21 Stop (close < EMA21) (active on L1, L2 and L3 before any L1 fills) using the per-leg ema_exit_period column.

PnL by Recent 30m EMA Cross

"Yes" = a bullish 30-min EMA cross (fast over slow) happened within the last 130 30-min bars at the moment of entry. Toggle ENABLE_RECENT_30M_CROSS_FILTER in bt_config.py to make this a hard entry filter.

MFE vs Realised Return (Capture Efficiency)

Efficiency = realised% / MFE% for winning trades. 100% = caught the entire move. Lower = leaving money on the table.

Realised return vs MFE
StatValue
Trades w/ MFE > 042,952
Mean efficiency (winners only)68.04%
Median efficiency (winners only)79.07%

What-If Target Sweep

For each multiplier m, target_pct = (m − 1) × 100%. A trade is assumed to fill at the target if its MFE ≥ target_pct, otherwise the actual realised return is used. This is a useful first-cut sanity check; real partial-exit logic with bar sequencing may differ slightly.
